pukkusakule.meaning: in a refuse-cleaner's clan; in a family of the pukkusa (scavenger) caste
breakdown: In a clan, family (locative of kula).
pukkusakule.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entry (puggalo “individual”); only “refuse-cleaner + clan” belongs to pukkusakule.

punappunaṁ.gram: indeclinable adverb (puna + puna, with sandhi doubling)

punareti.gram: compound verb, 3rd person singular present (puna + eti, with linking r)
breakdown: Linking consonant at the vowel boundary.

puppekatahetu.meaning: by reason of deeds done previously; because of past actions
puppekatahetu.note: uncertain: the printed form puppe- appears to stand for pubbe- (pubbekatahetu, the view that all experience is caused by past deeds).

purakkhatvā.meaning: having put in front; having honoured, placed before oneself
breakdown: Having made/done (from karoti, with sandhi doubling of k): “having placed in front”.

purato.gram: indeclinable adverb / ablative of pura used adverbially

purisa-yugāni.meaning: pairs of men (the four pairs of noble persons)
purisa-yugāni.summary: From the stock praise of the Saṅgha: cattāri purisayugāni, “the four pairs of persons” who have reached the stages of the path and their fruits.

purisadammasārathi.meaning: trainer of men to be tamed; charioteer of the human beings who need taming (an epithet of the Buddha)
breakdown: To be tamed (future passive participle related to dameti “to tame”).
purisadammasārathi.goenka: Purisadammasārathi is one of the nine qualities of the Buddha recited in the stock formula iti'pi so bhagavā: he is the incomparable trainer of those who are fit to be tamed, guiding beings out of their unwholesome conditioning the way a skilled charioteer masters his horses.

purisakāraṁ.meaning: manly deed; human effort, the act of a man

purisapuggalassa.meaning: of a person; of an individual man

purisādhame.meaning: the vilest of men; the lowest, meanest person
purisādhame.gram: masculine noun, compound, locative singular (or accusative plural)

puthujjana.meaning: common person; worldling (one who has not reached any stage of liberation)
breakdown: Person; sandhi doubling of j: the “many-folk”, the ordinary unliberated person.
puthujjana.goenka: A puthujjana is the ordinary worldling who has not yet experienced nibbāna — one who keeps reacting blindly to sensations with craving and aversion, multiplying saṅkhāra. The path of vipassana is the way by which a puthujjana becomes an ariya, a noble one, through the direct experience of the truth within.

puthukāyā.meaning: numerous in body; with separate, individual forms

puttadārassa.meaning: of children and wife; of one's family

puttasambādhasayanaṁ.meaning: a bed crowded with children; the household life hemmed in by sons

put­.meaning: (fragment of puttasambādhasayanaṁ) a bed crowded with children
put­.gram: glossary artifact; line-broken fragment of the compound puttasambādhasayanaṁ
put­.note: uncertain: the headword is a hyphenation fragment; see the entry puttasambādhasayanaṁ.

puññabhāgiyo.meaning: connected with merit; conducive to merit
breakdown: Sharing in, connected with, conducive to.

puññabhāgiyā.meaning: connected with merit; conducive to merit (plural/feminine)
puññabhāgiyā.gram: adjective puññabhāgiya, nominative plural or feminine singular

puññakammasampattiṁ.meaning: the attainment resulting from meritorious actions

puññakkhettaṁ.summary: From the stock praise of the Saṅgha: anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa, “the incomparable field of merit for the world”.
breakdown: Field; sandhi doubling of k. As seeds planted in a fertile field yield abundantly, so do wholesome deeds done towards the Saṅgha.

puṇṇamāya.meaning: on the full-moon (night); of the full moon

puṇṇamāya made.gram: glossary artifact; feminine noun puṇṇamā, locative/genitive singular
puṇṇamāya made.note: uncertain: the headword is a run-on glossary line for puṇṇamāya “(on the night) of the full moon”.

puṇṇāya.gram: past participle puṇṇa (of pūrati), feminine oblique singular

puṭosenāpi.meaning: even with a provision bag; moreover with a food container (for the journey)
breakdown: Per the lexicon, associated with carrying; puṭosa is a provision bag.
Even, moreover (api > pi, fused at the boundary).
puṭosenāpi.note: uncertain: the lexicon parses puṭo + aṁsa + api while DPD reads puṭa + osa; the sense “(travelling) even with provision bag” is common to both.

breakdown: The niggahīta joins the following vowel as m.

pābhataṁ.gram: past participle of pa + ā + √bhar used as neuter noun, nominative/accusative singular
breakdown: Root meaning “to carry”: “what is brought along”, hence capital, a gift.

pācīnaninnā pācīnapoṇā pācīnapabbhārā.meaning: bending towards the east, sloping towards the east, inclining towards the east
pācīnaninnā pācīnapoṇā pācīnapabbhārā.gram: phrase: three parallel compound adjectives, nominative plural
pācīnaninnā pācīnapoṇā pācīnapabbhārā.summary: A stock simile: as rivers bend, slope and incline towards the east, so the practitioner of the path bends, slopes and inclines towards nibbāna.

pāde.gram: masculine noun pāda, accusative plural or locative singular
breakdown: Root meaning “to step, to go” (lengthened to pād): the foot.
Root meaning “to offer”: one to whom offering/hospitality is due, a guest.
Adjectival suffix “worthy of”: deserving the offerings made to guests — said of the Saṅgha.

pāhuneyyā worthy of.gram: glossary artifact; adjective pāhuneyya, nominative plural
pāhuneyyā worthy of.note: uncertain: the headword is a run-on glossary line for pāhuneyyā “worthy of hospitality”.

pāli.meaning: Pāli (the language of the texts, Pāli bhāsā); the canonical texts maintained in the Pāli Tipiṭaka
pāli.gram: feminine noun; proper noun as language name
pāli.goenka: In this collection, Pāli is taken as Pāli bhāsā — the language of the texts preserved in the Pāli Tipiṭaka, valued as the medium in which the Buddha's words are maintained. The question of the language's historical origin — its development from the Indo-Aryan languages of Māgadhī and/or Prakrit and Sanskrit — is deliberately left aside.

breakdown: Abstract noun suffix: the state of grey hair, a mark of ageing.

pāna.meaning: drink; beverage (in the compound appāhārapānabhojana: little food, drink and nourishment)
pāna.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line carries a larger compound (… + pāna + bhojane “little + food + drink + nourishment”); only “drink” belongs to pāna.

pāpakaṁpissa.meaning: and even of his evil (deed); evil-doing too, of him
pāpakaṁpissa.gram: sandhi cluster: adjective pāpakaṁ + particle pi + genitive assa
breakdown: Of him, his (genitive); pi + assa fuse to pissa.
pāpakaṁpissa.note: uncertain: the lexicon parses pāpakamma + pi + issa; the more regular reading is pāpakaṁ + pi + assa.

pāpamariyehi.meaning: (what is) evil by the noble ones; evil in the eyes of the noble ones
pāpamariyehi.gram: compound phrase: pāpa + ariyehi (instrumental plural), with linking m
breakdown: Linking consonant at the vowel boundary.
By the noble ones (instrumental plural of ariya).

pāpasampavaṅko.meaning: inclined towards evil companions; a friend of the wicked
breakdown: Companion, intimate, one who leans towards.

pāpeti.meaning: causes to attain; makes reach, lets go to
pāpeti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of pāpuṇāti (pa + √ap)
breakdown: Causative stem of √ap “to reach”: “cause to reach”.

pāragu.meaning: gone beyond; one who has crossed over (to the far shore)
pāragu.goenka: “Gone beyond” describes one who has crossed the flood of saṁsāra to the far shore of nibbāna. In the tradition's imagery the practice of the path is the crossing: the meditator works patiently and persistently until the ocean of dukkha is fully traversed.
